cv_text:
## Mahdieh Heydarigoojani

<EMAIL> | <PHONE> | <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>

## Professional Summary

Multidisciplinary Biomedical Engineer with a solid background in biomaterials processing, specialized in designing and manufacturing hydrogel-based systems for cell and drug delivery. Experience in medical devices manufacturing including product design and quality assurance to ensure compliance and performance. Keen on identifying and analyzing challenges and collaborating with cross-functional teams to support material processing and advance innovative drug delivery systems from concept to clinical application.

## Core Expertise

- Skilled in biomaterials characterization techniques including  1 H-NMR, AFM, DLS, GPC, rheology, and unconfined compression testing
- Over 6 years of experience in mammalian cell culture using 2D and 3D systems (stem and primary cells)
- Experience with cell and tissue characterization techniques such as cell viability/proliferation assays, ELISA, flow cytometry, and IHC
- Deep knowledge of working with advanced manufacturing tools such as 3D bioprinting technology
- Proficient in software tools including AutoCAD, <CANDIDATE_NAME> (G-coding for 3D models), <LOCATION>, ImageJ, GraphPad Prism for data analysis and modeling

## Professional Experience

## 2020 -2025: Researcher at University of Ottawa (<LOCATION>, <LOCATION>)

- Designed and developed novel tissue substitutes using 3D bioprinting to address osteoarthritis
- Designed and synthesized hydrogels for the fabrication of tissue substitutes
- Conducted comprehensive physicochemical assessment of hydrogels using techniques including  1 H-NMR, rheology and mechanical testing
- Achieved significantly improved cell functions including viability, proliferation and differentiation
- Troubleshot bioprocess challenge by integrating an effective antifungal reagent into the bioreactor system, improving sterility and culture reliability
- Presented scientific results to a larger audience in national and international conferences
- Authored and documented peer-reviewed articles and SOP

## 2018 -2019: Researcher at Ludwig Boltzmann Institute (LBI, <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>)

- Investigated the effect of TRAP-6 on vascular network formation in a cell-laden hydrogel system
- Designed and optimized co-culture condition of stem and primary cells
- Extensively assessed vascular network formation through in vitro analysis
- Optimized  a  methodological  approach  for  vascular  network  quantification  in  fluorescence  microscopy images

2018:  Research trainee at CellChip Group, Vienna University of Technology (4-month internship) (<LOCATION>, <LOCATION>)

- Manufactured vascular-on-a-chip model for drug screening applications
- Developed a method to transfer cells with maximized post-transfer viability
- Optimized the design and manufacturing of the microfluidic device to reach a laminar flow (collaborated with a team of engineers and biochemists)

## 2016 -2017: Application Scientist manufacturing dental implants with Kimia Kasht (<LOCATION>, <LOCATION>)

- Conducted detailed product training sessions for over 50 healthcare professionals
- Collaborated with a cross-functional team to resolve issues related to implant dimensions and enhance clinical performance
- Conferred with customers (dentists) to ensure optimal requirements of implants are met

## 2014 -2015:  Research trainee at Nanobiotechnology Group, Tarbiat Modarres University (one-year internship) (<LOCATION>, <LOCATION>)

- Formulated nanoparticles designed for drug delivery with application in cardiac stents
- Demonstrated potential to reduce post-stent complications
- Achieved sustained and controlled release of anti-inflammatory drugs

## Education

2020 -2025: Doctor of Philosophy in  Biomedical and Tissue  Engineering,   University of Ottawa (<LOCATION>, <LOCATION>)

2017 -2019: Master of Science in Biomaterials and Biomechanics, Vienna University of Technology (<LOCATION>, <LOCATION>),

2010 -2014: Bachelor of Science in Biomedical Engineering, specialized in Biomaterials, <CANDIDATE_NAME> (<LOCATION>, <LOCATION>

## Honors & Awards

- Best second oral presentation award at the 'Additive Manufacturing for Health (AMH) Conference, March 2024
- Second place award at the 'Enabling Technologies for HealthCare and Augmented Life, poster competition', March 2023 & 2024
- <CANDIDATE_NAME>, MD FRCSC Graduate Fellowship, Issued by University of Ottawa, April 2023

## Leadership Roles

- Student Board Representative, Canadian Biomaterials Society (CBS) | 2024 -Present
- President, CBS-Ottawa Student Chapter | 2022 -2024
- Member, International Society for Biofabrication | 2023 -2024

## Communications and Selected Publications

- M.Heydarigoojani , E.A. Lehoux, I. Catelas, 'Engineering of a gradient multilayered osteochondral tissue construct using spatially defined bioinks: Part A: Bioinks development ', under -review
- M.Heydarigoojani , E.A. Lehoux, I. Catelas, ' Engineering of a gradient multilayered osteochondral tissue construct using spatially defined bioinks: Part B: 3D bioprinting gradient construct, under review

- M.Heydarigoojani, <CANDIDATE_NAME>, <CANDIDATE_NAME>, 'Bioinks for engineering gradient-based osteochondral and meniscal tissue substitutes: a review', <CANDIDATE_NAME>, 2025
- M.Heydarigoojani , E.A. Lehoux, I. Catelas, 'Evaluation of the cytocompatibility and bioactivity of new bioinks for 3D bioprinting of a multilayered osteochondral tissue substitute'. Additive Manufacturing for Health (AMH) conference, March 21-22, 2024, <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>
- M.Heydarigoojani , E.A. Lehoux, I. Catelas, '<CANDIDATE_NAME> -guided spatiotemporal growth factor release for osteochondral tissue engineering'. International Conference in <LOCATION>, September 17-20, 2023, <LOCATION>, <LOCATION>
- M.Heydarigoojani , E.A. Lehoux, I. Catelas, 'Development of bioinks for 3D bioprinting of an osteochondral tissue s ubstitute'. Society for Biomaterials (SFB) 2023 Annual Meeting and Exposition, April 19-22, 2023, <LOCATION>, CA

References provided upon request
pitch_text:
## Document 2: Assignment 4 (Pitch)

Selected Job Offer Position: Process Development Scientist (Target: Morphocell Technologies or similar) Key Requirements:

- PhD in Biomedical Engineering or related field.
- Experience with cell culture, bioreactors, and biomaterial formulation.
- Ability to troubleshoot scale-up processes.
- Strong communication and presentation skills.

## The Elevator Pitch

"Hi, I'm <CANDIDATE_NAME>. I am a Biomedical Engineer who specializes in turning fragile biological ideas into robust, manufacturable products .

During my PhD at the University of Ottawa, I didn't just grow cells; I engineered entire 3D-printed tissue environments . I formulated novel hydrogels, optimized their printing parameters for structural integrity, and successfully integrated them into bioreactors to grow functional osteochondral tissue. I know exactly how tricky it is to balance 'biological viability' with 'mechanical strength' because I've spent the last 5 years solving that exact trade-off.

I also bring industry experience from my time as an Application Scientist, where I learned that the best technology is useless if it doesn't fit the user's workflow. I recently received positive feedback for my structured approach to problem-solving in a similar role, and I'm eager to bring that same mix of technical rigor and manufacturing mindset to your process development team to help scale your cell therapies."

clarify_text.ikigai:
My Ikigai is engineering living solutions for complex medical challenges .

The central theme of my career is "Structure and Function." Whether it was formulating nanoparticles for cardiac stents or designing 3D-bioprinted osteochondral tissues, I am passionate about creating materials that mimic biology . My talent lies in the intersection of material science (formulation, rheology) and advanced manufacturing (3D bioprinting). The world needs scalable, effective tissue substitutes to treat aging populations and injuries, a need I am uniquely positioned to meet.

+2

I can create a lasting impact by developing bio-manufacturable products that move from the lab to the clinic, reducing the gap between "promising research" and "actual patient cure." To align my career with this, I am targeting roles in Process Development and R&D where I can apply my bioprinting expertise to industrial-scale problems. I am ready to transition from the exploratory nature of academia to the rigorous, timeline-driven environment of biotech product development.
clarify_text.ideal_day:
My ideal day is a dynamic mix of hands-on innovation and collaborative problem-solving .

It starts in the lab, overseeing a critical run on a 3D bioprinter. I am optimizing the print parameters for a new hydrogel formulation, checking the rheology data to ensure printability.

By mid-day, I transition to a team meeting with Quality Assurance and Manufacturing engineers. We are discussing how to scale up my bench-top process to a sterile, GMP-compliant workflow. I enjoy translating my technical findings into actionable process improvements. The afternoon involves analyzing cell viability data from yesterday's print using ImageJ , ensuring the cells survived the manufacturing process. The day ends with a sense of tangible progress-a physical prototype that works better than the one from yesterday.
clarify_text.smart_goals:
- Goal 1 (Career Placement): Secure a full-time position as a Process Development Scientist or R&D Engineer in a Canadian biotech company (e.g., Ripple Therapeutics, Morphocell) by June 2025.
- Measurable: Submit 5 tailored applications weekly; follow up on my recent successful interview with the Process Development team.
- Attainable: Recent positive feedback confirms my profile is competitive.
- Goal 2 (Scientific Dissemination): Publish my two papers currently under review regarding "Gradient Multilayered Osteochondral Tissue Constructs" by December 2025.
- Measurable: Manuscripts accepted and published in peer-reviewed journals.
- Realistic: Papers are already written and under review; goal focuses on addressing reviewer comments.
- Goal 3 (Professional Networking): Expand my network in the "Cell & Gene Therapy" manufacturing space by connecting with 20 industry professionals on LinkedIn by May 2025.
- Measurable: Send 5 personalized invites per week; schedule 2 informational interviews.

- Time-bound: 3 months.

preferences.target_companies: 1. Ripple Therapeutics, 2. Morphocell Technologies, 3. Aspect Biosystems
preferences.application_history: I have applied to several biotechnology companies in Canada whose research and development focus aligns with my background in biomaterials, tissue engineering, and cell therapy. These include Ripple Therapeutics and Morphocell Technologies.
preferences.recent_interviews: Yes, for the Process Development Scientist positionwhere I delivered a technical presentation highlighting my experience in biomaterials formulation, bioprocess optimization, and cell-based product development. The feedback was very positive — the team appreciated the clarity of my presentation, the relevance of my technical background to their current projects, and my structured approach to problem-solving.
